zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Margaret De Montgomerie Mure of Rowallan於1235年出生在蘇格蘭雷福福爾易阿格萊善姆時，她的父親John Montgomery "Miles," of Eaglesham 3rd Laird of Eaglesham11歲，母親Margaret Moray of Bothwell15歲。她於1254年與Sir Archibald Mure 2nd Lord Rowallan在蘇格蘭雷福福爾伊斯威特結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1275年在蘇格蘭艾斯爾吉爾馬爾諾可享年40歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭艾斯爾吉爾馬爾諾可。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady Margaret De Montgomerie Mure of Rowallan于1235年出生在苏格兰雷福福爾易阿格萊善姆时，她的父亲John Montgomery "Miles," of Eaglesham 3rd Laird of Eaglesham11岁，母亲Margaret Moray of Bothwell15岁。她于1254年与Sir Archibald Mure 2nd Lord Rowallan在苏格兰雷福福爾伊斯威特结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1275年在苏格兰艾斯爾吉爾馬爾諾可享年40岁，埋葬在苏格兰艾斯爾吉爾馬爾諾可。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Montgomerie
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Norman, Scottish: Scottish spelling of   Montgomery  .

variants.name: Montgomery
meanings: English, Scottish, and northern Irish (of Norman origin): habitational name from Sainte-Foy-de-Montgomery and Saint-Germain-de-Montgomery (Calvados). In Ireland this surname was present in the medieval period, died out, and was then reintroduced from Scotland in the 17th century. It has been Gaelicized in Ireland as Mac Iomaire and in Scotland as Mac Gumaraid.

nameMeanings.name: Mure
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Italian (Sicily): variant of   Morea  .
Scottish: variant of   Muir  .
English: occupational name from Old French muer ‘mewer’, i.e. someone who tended to hawks in the cages in which they were kept while moulting. Compare Mew .

variants.name: Mura
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Japanese Akio, Hideo, Masao, Nanako, Tsutomu, Yoshi.  French Alphonse, Amie, Henri.
German: altered form of some similar (like-sounding) German or perhaps French surname. It is also common in France (Alsace), where it was first recorded in the form Morrau, later Murra(w).
Italian (southern Sardinia): topographic name or nickname from Sardinian mura ‘mulberry’ or ‘blackberry’.

variants.name: Muir
meanings: Scottish: topographic name for someone who lived on a moor, from Older Scots muir ‘moor’ (an expanse of bare, wild upland country). There has been some confusion with Mure .

variants.name: Morea
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Saverio, Domenic, Ferdinando, Rocco.  Spanish Manuel, Carlos, Cesar, Elvira, Jesus, Mauricio.

Italian (Apulia): from the personal name Morea, originally a habitational name from Morea, the medieval name of the Greek region of Peloponnese, named as the land of mulberry trees, from mōrea ‘mulberry’.
Spanish and Catalan: habitational name from any of the places called Morea, in Badajoz, Navarre, and Lleida provinces.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: possibly a metonymic occupational name for a fisherman, from orfe, a species of freshwater fish. Alternatively, a habitational name from Urff near Kassel, formerly Orpha.

variants.name: Orff
meanings: German: variant of   Orf  .

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Margaret De Montgomerie Mure of Rowallan was born in 1235, in Eaglesham, Renfrewshire, Scotland, her father, John Montgomery "Miles," of Eaglesham 3rd Laird of Eaglesham, was 11 and her mother, Margaret Moray of Bothwell, was 15. She married Sir Archibald Mure 2nd Lord Rowallan in 1254, in Eastwood, Renfrewshire, Scotland. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 3 daughters. She died in 1275, in Kilmarnock, Ayrshire, Scotland, at the age of 40, and was buried in Kilmarnock, Ayrshire, Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Margaret De Montgomerie Mure of Rowallan est née en 1235, à Eaglesham, Renfrewshire, Écosse, son père, John Montgomery "Miles," of Eaglesham 3rd Laird of Eaglesham, avait 11 ans et sa mère, Margaret Moray of Bothwell, avait 15 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Archibald Mure 2nd Lord Rowallan en 1254, à Eastwood, Renfrewshire, Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée en 1275, à Kilmarnock, Ayrshire, Écosse, à l’âge de 40 ans, et a été enterrée à Kilmarnock, Ayrshire, Écosse.

de.lifeSummary: Als Lady Margaret De Montgomerie Mure of Rowallan 1235 in Eaglesham, Renfrewshire, Scotland geboren wurde, war ihr Vater John Montgomery "Miles," of Eaglesham 3rd Laird of Eaglesham 11 und ihre Mutter Margaret Moray of Bothwell 15. Sie hat 1254 in Eastwood, Renfrewshire, Scotland Sir Archibald Mure 2nd Lord Rowallan geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb 1275 im Alter von 40 Jahren in Kilmarnock, Ayrshire, Scotland und wurde in Kilmarnock, Ayrshire, Scotland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Margaret De Montgomerie Mure of Rowallan è nata il nel 1235 nel Eaglesham, Renfrewshire, Scotland, sua padre, John Montgomery "Miles," of Eaglesham 3rd Laird of Eaglesham, aveva 11 anni e sua madre, Margaret Moray of Bothwell, aveva 15 anni. Lei sposato Sir Archibald Mure 2nd Lord Rowallan nel 1254, nel Eastwood, Renfrewshire, Scotland. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 3 figlie. Morì nel 1275, nel Kilmarnock, Ayrshire, Scotland, all'età di 40 anni, e fu sepolta a Kilmarnock, Ayrshire, Scotland.

ja.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret De Montgomerie Mure of Rowallanが1235年にスコットランドレンフリューシュアイアエアグレシャムで生まれた時，父John Montgomery "Miles," of Eaglesham 3rd Laird of Eagleshamは11歳，母Margaret Moray of Bothwellは15歳であった。1254年にスコットランドレンフリューシュアイアエアストウッドでSir Archibald Mure 2nd Lord Rowallanと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1275年にスコットランドエイルシュアイアキルマルノックで40歳で死去し，スコットランドエイルシュアイアキルマルノックにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret De Montgomerie Mure of Rowallan(이)가 1235년 스컷랜드 렌프레w실르 이글레샤에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Montgomery "Miles," of Eaglesham 3rd Laird of Eaglesham(은)는 11세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margaret Moray of Bothwell(은)는 15세였다. 그녀는 1254년 스컷랜드 렌프레w실르 이스트우드에서 Sir Archibald Mure 2nd Lord Rowallan(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1275년 스컷랜드 에이르실르 킬마노크에서 40세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드 에이르실르 킬마노크에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Margaret De Montgomerie Mure of Rowallan nasceu em 1235, em Eaglesham, Renfrewshire, Scotland, seu pai, John Montgomery "Miles," of Eaglesham 3rd Laird of Eaglesham, tinha 11 anos e sua mãe, Margaret Moray of Bothwell, tinha 15 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir Archibald Mure 2nd Lord Rowallan em 1254, em Eastwood, Renfrewshire, Scotland. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 3 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1275, em Kilmarnock, Ayrshire, Scotland, com 40 anos, e foi sepultada em Kilmarnock, Ayrshire, Scotland.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Margaret De Montgomerie Mure of Rowallan родилась в 1235 г. в местности эаглешам, ренфрюшир, Шотландия, её отцу по имени John Montgomery "Miles," of Eaglesham 3rd Laird of Eaglesham было 11 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret Moray of Bothwell, 15. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Archibald Mure 2nd Lord Rowallan в 1254 г. в местности эаствуд, ренфрюшир, Шотландия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 3 дочери. Она умерла в 1275 г. в местности килмарнокк, аиршир, Шотландия в возрасте 40 лет и была похоронена в местности килмарнокк, аиршир, Шотландия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Lady Margaret De Montgomerie Mure of Rowallan nació en 1235, en Eaglesham, Renfrewshire, Scotland, su papá, John Montgomery "Miles," of Eaglesham 3rd Laird of Eaglesham, tenía 11 años y su mamá, Margaret Moray of Bothwell, tenía 15 años. Se casó con Sir Archibald Mure 2nd Lord Rowallan en 1254, en Eastwood, Renfrewshire, Scotland. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió en 1275, en Kilmarnock, Ayrshire, Scotland, a la edad de 40 años, y fue sepultada en Kilmarnock, Ayrshire, Scotland.
